title: Forward-Only Latent Degradation State
summary: A new F1 tire-degradation paper models wear as a latent state with explicit pit-reset dynamics and reports better predictive behavior than simpler autoregressive baselines.
rationale: A new F1 tire-degradation paper models wear as a latent state with explicit pit-reset dynamics and reports better predictive behavior than simpler autoregressive baselines. That fits this dataset well because observed `Cumulative_Degradation` and lap-time noise likely under-measure the true stint state.
implementation_hint: Fit a lightweight forward-only filter per Year/Race/Driver/Stint, using past `LapTime_Delta` and `Cumulative_Degradation` to update a latent wear level and slope, with reset after pit. Emit posterior wear, posterior slope, and innovation/shock as new features. Keep it filtering-only, not two-sided smoothing.
expected_effect: Should reduce sensitivity to one-off slow laps and create cleaner pit-window signals, especially in noisy or mixed-condition stints.
risk: Any backward smoothing leaks future information; an over-parameterized filter may just repackage noise and add instability.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
